raw_consumer_statement:
Called XXXX XXXX  and requested specialty dinner for XXXX passengers within my group. Was informed by XXXX agent should could only make XXXX reservations at a time and that we would need to make XXXX reservations. The XXXX agent said she could do that. We proceeded and made the initial two reservations and charged my credit card 2 times ( with my approval ). When making the third reservation she informed me the restaurant was now sold out and she could not complete the reservation request. I asked her to cancel the 2 reservations that were made and she stated she had ands the charges would be reversed. 

The credits never appeared and I contacted Citibank and disputed the charges. Citibank contacted the merchant and the merchant did not present any documentation. After two attempts Citibank stated since the merchant did not respond no credit / charge reversal could be completed. I have requested documentation and none can be provided. XXXX claims to record all conversation for security and accuracy reasons and should be able to confirm the conversation. 

Citibank writes : " We have made every attempt to resolve your dispute with the merchant. They are unwilling to issue a credit to your account and have informed us that this charge is valid. While we can understand your desire to obtain a credit to your account, we can not mandate that the merchant issue a credit outside of their terms and conditions. Therefore, we have no recourse available to recover the funds. However, if you wish to pursue this matter, you may contact the merchant directly to resolve. '' The merchant can not just say it is valid, they must offer documentation.
